fiddleheads:gamemechanicinfo-fiddleheads:
Matteuccia struthiopteris

Often called ostrich fern, fiddlehead fern or shuttlecock fern, are one of very few ferns that are edible when cooked.

Fiddleheads are the furled fronds, harvested for use as a vegetable.

Fiddleheads are harvested early in the season, before the frond has opened and reached its full height, they are cut fairly close to the ground.

Seasonal Availability:
Fiddleheads are available seasonally, typically in spring. They are a perenial plant, and will regrow in the same area they are found in the wild, and they're worth marking on your map.

Fiddleheads are both foraged and cultivated, and harvested in spring. It is important to harvest fiddleheads at the right time to ensure the best flavor and texture. The early growth stages are best for havesting the edible furled fronds, as leaving the plant for too long will just grow into a large fern plant.

Stage 1: Initial growth stage, nothing to harvest.
Stage 2: Furled fronds starting to appear, can be harvested for a small amount.
Stage 3: More furled fronds appear, can be harvested for a larger amount.
Stage 4: Primary stage to harvest, for best yield of furled fronds.
Stage 5: Last stage that will yield a small amount of edible furled fronds.
Stage 6-9: These stages will only yield fully grown fronds.
